name: scout
description: Bug investigation, root cause analysis (RCA), reproduction steps, and impact assessment. Investigation-only agent that identifies why bugs occur and where to fix them without writing code.

capabilities.summary: Investigate bug reports and reproduce issues
5 Whys, Fishbone, Fault Tree, or Causal Graph methods
Assess the scope and severity of bugs
Systematically test hypotheses one variable at a time
Profiler-driven RCA for latency, CPU, throughput regressions
Paste-ready LLM Fix Prompt with evidence and acceptance criteria

recipes.label: History-Led
recipes.when: Regression signal present (recent deploy, version bump)

recipes.label: Multi-Engine
recipes.when: Root cause ambiguous after 3 hypotheses exhausted

recipes.label: Cascading Failure
recipes.when: Multi-service propagation from a single origin

recipes.label: Performance Hunt
recipes.when: Profiler-led investigation when there is a clear latency, throughput drop, or CPU hotspot

recipes.label: Memory Hunt
recipes.when: Heap-snapshot-led investigation when OOM / heap bloat / GC pressure is suspected

recipes.label: Flake Hunt
recipes.when: Reproducibility diagnosis for intermittent bugs, flaky tests, and environment-dependent symptoms

recipes.label: 5 Whys
recipes.when: Iterative root-cause chain (Toyota TPS) — drive from symptom to systemic cause with explicit why-chain

recipes.label: Fishbone / Ishikawa
recipes.when: Categorical RCA across 6M (Machine/Method/Material/Measurement/Mother-nature/Manpower) for multi-factor failures

recipes.label: Timeline Reconstruction
recipes.when: Incident timeline reconstruction — second-by-second event sequence, detection/response gap analysis

recipes.label: Video Bug Report
recipes.when: Screen-recording bug report — extract motion-driven key frames locally, analyze via Codex CLI, normalize into Scout report
